name: Zubin Mehta
abstract: Zubin Mehta (born 29 April 1936) is an Indian conductor of Western classical music. He is music director emeritus of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra (IPO) and conductor emeritus of the Los Angeles Philharmonic.

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Centennial Festival: Carnegie Hall Centennial Gala Concert

works.t: Third Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman
Variations on a Rococo Theme, Op. 33
Sinfonia Concertante in E-flat Major, K. 364: 1. Allegro maestoso

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Centennial Festival: Carnegie Hall Centennial Gala Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I had started writing my number for Zubin Mehta and for the philharmonic, you know. So he said, “what is that you’re working on, Gerald?” I said, “listen, this is a number that the Los Angeles Philharmonic is going to play,”
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I composed a number that they performed, and then I later orchestrated six other things for them and Zubin Mehta conducted all of my numbers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: At the time, Zarin Mehta, the brother of Zubin Mehta, was the director of the whole festival.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I remember a time sitting – when I played with the Philharmonic... then came Zubin Mehta.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
